task_completion.description: The agent successfully logged into the Floreant POS system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main dashboard or order type selection screen of Floreant POS is visible, indicating the agent bypassed the initial login screen (default PIN 1111).

task_completion.description: The agent initiated an order specifically for Table 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active order screen or ticket shows 'Table 1' (or 'TBL 1') as the selected table for the current transaction.

task_completion.description: The agent added the first menu item to the order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The order ticket/receipt area on the POS interface displays at least one food or drink item (e.g., 'HAMMER COFFEE' or another menu item).

task_completion.description: The agent added a second menu item to the order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The order ticket/receipt area on the POS interface displays a second food or drink item, bringing the total number of items in the current order to at least two.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully placed or finished the order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation screen, receipt dialog, payment screen completion, or a return to the main dashboard/table layout showing Table 1 as occupied/ordered, indicating the order was finalized via buttons like 'Finish', 'Pay', or 'Send'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface to place the order.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and navigation through the actual Floreant POS Java GUI, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to directly modify the POS database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible executing SQL commands, Derby database scripts, or direct file modifications to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Floreant POS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window displays the standard Floreant POS interface (Java Swing UI, typical restaurant POS layout) and not a mock text editor or web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target software is Floreant POS (version 1.4). The default login PIN for the POS system is '1111'. The task requires creating an order specifically for 'Table 1' and adding exactly or at least two menu items to the ticket before finalizing/placing the order. The database contains pre-configured items like 'HAMMER COFFEE'.
